zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Jane Cardinall於1406年出生在英國英格蘭艾可格亞特 布羅姆利時，她的父親William Cardinall39歲，母親Lady Lettice Knightley26歲。她於1425年與Sir Robert Vesey結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1439年在英國英格蘭艾可享年33歲。

nameMeanings.name: Cardinall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dante, Mario, Angelo, Sergio, Americo, Corinda, Emilio, Ennio, Gildo, Ilio, Luigi, Massimo, Romeo, Serafino, Sisto.

variants.name: Cardinale
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sal, Salvatore, Mario, Rocco, Vito, Angelo, Antonio, Cosimo, Salvador, Carlo, Cosmo, Francesca, Gasper, Gennaro, Angelina, Demetrio, Gaspar, Gerardo, Julio, Lucrecia, Romero, Rosario.
Italian: from cardinale ‘cardinal’ (see Cardinal ), a nickname for a haughty individual, or a metonymic occupational name for the servant of a cardinal.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Jane Cardinall was born in 1406, in Great Bromley, Essex, England, United Kingdom, her father, William Cardinall, was 39 and her mother, Lady Lettice Knightley, was 26. She married Sir Robert Vesey in 1425. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1439, in Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 33.

Elle a épousé Sir Robert Vesey en 1425. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1439, à Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 33 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Jane Cardinall(이)가 1406년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 브롬리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Cardinall(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Lettice Knightley(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1425년 Sir Robert Vesey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1439년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 33세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Jane Cardinall родилась в 1406 г. в местности грит бромли, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени William Cardinall было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Lettice Knightley, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Robert Vesey в 1425 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1439 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 33 лет.
